goal.objective: Write a decision-grade comparison note about Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3-Codex, anchored in official pages and strengthened with selective independent evidence.
goal.context:
## Research strategy

1. **Start with the official pages** — scrape the two primary URLs below for first-party claims.
2. **Build normalized comparison axes** before drafting conclusions (same dimensions for both models).
3. **Run selective web research** for independent benchmarks, developer analyses, and expert commentary that validate, contradict, or add nuance to major claims.
4. **Scrape additional pages only when they materially improve decision quality** (not for redundant coverage).

Always attribute every claim to its source. If a claim appears only in the vendor's own page and no independent source corroborates it, mark it as *"vendor-stated, not independently verified"*.
If direct apples-to-apples comparison is impossible for a metric, state that explicitly instead of inferring.

Stop collection when:
- All must_have sections have enough cited evidence to draft confidently.
- Additional pages are mostly repeating already captured claims.
- New sources do not change the decision implications.

## Required final structure

1. Executive takeaway
2. Side-by-side comparison (normalized axes)
3. Independent validation and contradictions
4. Developer implications ("best fit when...")
5. Caveats, unknowns, and non-comparable metrics
6. Evidence quality classification

## Primary source URLs (scrape these first)

- https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-6
- https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-3-codex/

## Suggested web search queries (non-exhaustive, agents may add more)

- "Claude Opus 4.6 benchmarks 2026"
- "GPT-5.3-Codex benchmarks 2026"
- "Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3 comparison"
- "Claude Opus 4.6 developer review"
- "GPT-5.3-Codex developer review"
must_have: A concise side-by-side comparison of key claims using normalized axes (capabilities, benchmarks, long-context or agentic features, and product/API updates when stated).
Independent corroboration or contrast for major vendor claims, and explicit "not independently verified" labeling where corroboration is missing.
A practical "what this means for developers" section with "best fit when..." guidance and caveats about non-comparable metrics.
An evidence quality section that tags major conclusions as vendor-stated, independently-supported, or mixed.

Final markdown note (target 900-1300 words) at report/final-report.md. Every factual claim must be cited using short markdown links — e.g. ([Anthropic docs](url)) — never raw URLs. Collect all sources in a numbered "Sources" section at the end of the report.
HTML version of the final report at deliverables/report.html, rendered using the project template (render_html tool).
forbidden: Hype language, fanboy framing, or unsupported superiority claims
Presenting a single benchmark result without naming the source and methodology
Mixing up which claim comes from which source
Inferring superiority from non-comparable metrics without an explicit caveat

project.title: Decision-Grade Comparison: Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3-Codex
project.description: A rigorously sourced, decision-grade comparison note between Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3-Codex, anchored in official documentation and strengthened with selective independent evidence, for developer and technical decision-maker audiences.

tasks.title: Collect and Structure Evidence with Source Traceability
tasks.body: Scrape the official pages for Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3-Codex. Extract all major claims, benchmarks, and product/API updates. Run targeted web searches for independent benchmarks, developer reviews, and expert commentary that validate or contradict vendor claims. Structure all findings in a JSON file with explicit source attribution (short markdown links), tagging each claim as 'vendor-stated', 'independently-supported', or 'mixed'. Mark claims as 'not independently verified' where applicable. Output: evidence/structured-evidence.json for downstream synthesis.
successCriteria: All major claims from both official pages are extracted and attributed.
At least three independent sources per model are included if available.
Each claim is tagged for evidence quality and verification status.
All sources are cited using short markdown links.
Output is a well-structured JSON file ready for synthesis.

tasks.title: Build Normalized Comparison Matrix
tasks.body: Using evidence/structured-evidence.json, construct a side-by-side comparison matrix with normalized axes: capabilities, benchmarks, long-context/agentic features, and product/API updates. For each axis, present both models' claims, source attributions, and explicit notes on evidence quality (vendor-stated, independently-supported, mixed, or not independently verified). Output: analysis/comparison-matrix.md for use in the draft.
successCriteria: Matrix covers all required axes for both models.
Each claim is attributed and tagged for evidence quality.
Non-comparable metrics are explicitly noted.
Output is a markdown table or equivalent, ready for synthesis.

tasks.title: Draft Decision-Grade Comparison Note
tasks.body: Write a 900-1300 word markdown report at drafts/draft-note.md, following the required structure: Executive takeaway, side-by-side comparison (using analysis/comparison-matrix.md), independent validation/contradictions, developer implications ('best fit when...'), caveats/unknowns/non-comparable metrics, and evidence quality classification. Every factual claim must be cited using short markdown links. Include a numbered 'Sources' section. Mark all unverified claims as 'not independently verified'.
successCriteria: Draft is within 900-1300 words.
All claims are cited with short markdown links.
All required sections are present and complete.
Comparison matrix is integrated and source attributions are clear.
Unverified claims are explicitly labeled.

tasks.title: Editorial Quality Gate: Citation and Caveat Verification
tasks.body: Review drafts/draft-note.md for citation integrity, correct attribution, and explicit caveats for non-comparable metrics. Ensure all forbidden elements are absent. Refine language for clarity and neutrality. Output: drafts/edited-note.md for final assembly.
successCriteria: All claims are correctly attributed and cited.
All non-comparable metrics are explicitly caveated.
No forbidden content is present.
Language is neutral and decision-grade.

tasks.title: Assemble Final Markdown and HTML Deliverables
tasks.body: Copy drafts/edited-note.md to report/final-report.md. Use the render_html tool to produce report.html in deliverables/report.html, following the project template. Ensure all sources are included and formatting is consistent.
successCriteria: report/final-report.md is complete and matches editorial review.
deliverables/report.html is rendered and matches the markdown.
All sources are included and formatting is consistent.

assumptions: All required web pages and sources are accessible.
Sufficient independent evidence exists for at least partial validation.
The team will not exceed the max_total_tasks or step_budget_rounds.
risks: Some claims may lack independent corroboration, requiring explicit labeling.
Benchmarks may use non-comparable methodologies, necessitating careful caveats.
Time constraints may limit the depth of independent evidence collection.
